_meta.description: First-ever runWorkDayCycle() execution (month-1 launch session, 2026-06-11). Triggered via POST /api/agents/trigger {"type":"day"}. Result: HTTP 500 after 449s — Gemini API quota exceeded. This is a CHECKPOINT OF A FAILED/INCOMPLETE DAY 1, kept for diagnosis before retrying.

error.raw: Gemini API error (429): { "error": { "code": 429, "message": "You exceeded your current quota, please check your plan and billing details. For more information on this error, head to: https://ai.google.dev/gemini-api/docs/rate-limits. To monitor your current usage, head to: https://ai.dev/rate-limit. \n* Quota ex[...truncated by gemini-client.js's .slice(0,300)]"
error.diagnosis: GEMINI_API_KEY's Google AI Studio project hit a 429 quota/billing limit on gemini-2.5-flash-lite partway through day 1's case loop. Not a code bug — runWorkDayCycle and queryGemini both behaved correctly (no retry/backoff exists, so the first 429 propagated as a 500). CLAUDE.md assumes a 'paid' Gemini tier; this key appears to be on free-tier limits (or the daily free quota was already partly consumed by prior testing sessions).
error.action_required: User must check https://aistudio.google.com (or the linked Google Cloud project's billing) for the GEMINI_API_KEY, confirm gemini-2.5-flash-lite has a paid-tier quota, and either enable billing or wait for the free-tier daily quota to reset before re-running day 1.

cases.note: all 50 generated/persisted via persistCrmCases (INSERT OR IGNORE) before the loop started; status still 'open' for all (handleCase doesn't mark cases resolved yet — known DRAFT-status placeholder)

agent_sessions.note: 47 of 50 day-1 cases were processed by handleCase() before the 48th call's Gemini request hit 429

interactions.note: every interaction row = a real Claude (data-center-api -> claude-sonnet-4-6) call via the APP_API service binding. These succeeded fine — only Gemini hit quota.

reports.all: Status report — The Standard Agent (agent 3, status_report_chance=1.00)

year_stats.note: runWorkDayCycle failed before persistYearState/standup meeting ran, so current_day is still 0 — day 1 never officially completed.

estimated_cost_usd.claude_sonnet_4_6: ~0.10-0.50 (26 data-center-api calls, ~25 search + 1 diagnose; not metered precisely this session)
estimated_cost_usd.note: the 429 was a quota/rate limit, not a billing exhaustion -- no cost circuit-breaker concern. Spend is negligible.

admin_token.note: ADMIN_TOKEN was rotated via wrangler secret put as part of this launch session. New value given to the user directly in chat (not stored in repo). dc-admin-token in the dashboard's localStorage needs updating.

before_retrying_day_1: Resolve the Gemini quota/billing issue (see error.action_required above).

Decide how to handle the 47 partial sessions / 26 interactions / 10 reports already written for day 1: either (a) leave them and accept that a retried day-1 will reprocess all 50 cases as a second pass (since cases/year_stats aren't marked complete and persistCrmCases is idempotent via INSERT OR IGNORE, but agent.handleCase has no 'already done' check), producing duplicate sessions/interactions for cases 1-47, or (b) manually delete the day-1 partial rows from agent_sessions/interactions/reports/cases (crm-2026-w01-d1-*) for a clean retry.

Consider adding retry/backoff to agents/workers/gemini-client.js (e.g. exponential backoff on 429) and/or a per-case 'already processed today' guard in runWorkDayCycle, before the next attempt -- not done this session per the 'no behavior changes during the run' rule.
